summary: A minimal, responsive black-and-white game presskit website with ShadCN UI-inspired design. Features three routes: / (public landing), /press-kit (public or password-gated presskit), and /admin (Internet Identity-protected CMS dashboard). Internet Identity auth exists ONLY on /admin. The admin principal has been identified as r423e-lboyg-thldn-64zao-ew2h3-bh3v6-uouit-khwus-vkwn2-afrmu-rqe and will be hardcoded as the permanent admin in the backend. The temporarily open CMS will be locked down to only this principal.
architectural_notes: Internet Identity auth UI exists ONLY on /admin; no login UI on public pages
Password hashed with SHA-256 in backend; frontend never stores plaintext
getContent() returns all content + passwordEnabled flag; content gating must prevent flash on load
Dark mode: body text forced white, background/logo/festival logo inverted via CSS filter; admin-selected body text color only applies in light mode
Fredoka One font used for all header text site-wide
ZIP file PokeANose_MediaImages.zip must be served with correct MIME type for direct download
createActor must not receive both agent and agentOptions simultaneously; pass only the authenticated agent, never agentOptions alongside it
YouTube video URL stored in backend stable storage as a CMS field; press kit video section reads it from getContent()
Admin principal is permanently set to r423e-lboyg-thldn-64zao-ew2h3-bh3v6-uouit-khwus-vkwn2-afrmu-rqe; hardcoded in backend stable storage

existing_features.title: Set up React Router with three routes: / (Landing page), /press-kit (Press Kit page), /admin (Admin Dashboard). No authentication UI or login buttons exist on / or /press-kit. Navigation between pages uses standard router links.

Implement a global theme context (React Context + localStorage persistence) providing dark/light mode toggle. A toggle switch must appear in the header/navigation bar on public pages (/ and /press-kit). In dark mode: body text color is forced to white (#FFFFFF) overriding any admin-set color; background image gets CSS filter invert(1); gamelogo.png and kaboom-2025.png get CSS filter invert(1). In light mode: background image is normal; admin-set bodyTextColorHex applies to body text; logos display normally. Theme transitions must be smooth with CSS transitions and no flicker.

Implement a password gate modal component that appears on /press-kit when passwordEnabled is true. On app load, only the background image and the password modal are visible; all press kit content is hidden. The modal contains a password input field and an 'Access Presskit' button. On submit, call verifyPassword on the backend; if correct, hide modal and reveal content. If incorrect, show a clear inline error message. The modal must be theme-aware: light mode uses white background with dark text; dark mode uses dark background with white text and white inputs. Smooth CSS transitions apply when toggling theme while modal is open. Once password is verified in a session, content remains accessible without re-entering.

Build the public Landing Page (/). Layout: full-page background using background_web.jpg (with invert in dark mode). Center-aligned editorial layout. Include: (1) gamelogo.png displayed at significantly large size (min 400px wide on desktop), inverted in dark mode, normal in light mode; (2) a short description text paragraph; (3) a modular newsletter placeholder section with an email input field and a Subscribe button (non-functional placeholder, designed to be replaced later); (4) a prominent button/link navigating to /press-kit. Use 'Fredoka One' font for all heading/display text. Minimize vertical gaps between sections for a tight editorial feel.

Build the Press Kit Page (/press-kit) with the following sections in order: (1) Hero: game title in very large Fredoka One font and a tagline; (2) Video Section: display video_thumbnail.png uncropped with a centered play button overlay — on click, replace the image with an embedded YouTube iframe for https://youtu.be/5in-hIASH08; (3) About the Game: editable text from backend; (4) Features: up to 4 editable lines from backend, displayed directly under About; (5) Game Details: genre, platforms, release date from backend; (6) Media Section: 'Screenshots' sub-divider heading, a download button labeled 'Download all Screenshots as ZIP (6.6MB)' that triggers direct download of /assets/PokeANose_MediaImages.zip with correct application/zip MIME type, and a 6-image grid gallery (screen01.png through screen06.png) each with 1px solid black border; (7) Social Media: Instagram button (icon-only using /assets/generated/instagram-icon-transparent.dim_64x64.png), left-aligned, shown only if instagramLink is non-empty, opens in new tab, light mode = black background/white icon, dark mode = white background/black icon; (8) Developer section: developer website link; (9) Press & Support: press email; (10) Footer: kaboom-2025.png at increased size, inverted in dark mode, no section header label.

Implement a fullscreen screenshot viewer for the screenshots gallery on /press-kit. When a screenshot is clicked, it expands to cover the full viewport with all other UI hidden and page scrolling disabled. Clicking the fullscreen image again exits fullscreen and restores normal UI and scroll. The viewer must work reliably on tablets.

Build the Admin Dashboard (/admin) page with the following flow: (1) Show an Internet Identity login button (using useInternetIdentity hook) when the user is not authenticated; (2) After II authentication, call initializeAdmin() — if it returns success, display the full CMS editing dashboard; if it returns an access-denied error, display the error message and no CMS controls; (3) The CMS dashboard must contain editable fields for: About text (textarea), Features (up to 4 text inputs), Game Details (genre, platforms, release date text inputs), Instagram link (text input), Developer website (text input), Press email (text input), Body text color (color picker input, labeled as light mode only), and Password protection controls (toggle to enable/disable, password input field for setting/changing password). Each field has a Save button that calls the corresponding backend update method immediately. Password protection section shows current status (enabled/disabled) and allows admin to set/change/remove the password.

Apply global content state management using React Query to fetch getContent() on app load and share it across all pages. Admin saves must invalidate and refetch the content query so public pages reflect updates without requiring a manual refresh. The bodyTextColorHex from backend must be applied as a CSS custom property on the document root in light mode, and overridden to #FFFFFF in dark mode.

Apply a consistent minimalist black-and-white editorial visual theme across all pages. Use 'Fredoka One' (loaded from Google Fonts) for all headings and display text. Body font is a clean sans-serif. No decorative borders except the 1px black border on screenshots. Reduced vertical spacing between major sections. Fully responsive layout for desktop, tablet, and mobile. Smooth CSS transitions on all interactive elements. Robust image loading with fallback placeholder states. The overall aesthetic is inspired by ShadCN UI: clean, high-contrast, typographically strong.

Place all static assets in frontend/public/assets/: background_web.jpg, gamelogo.png, video_thumbnail.png, screen01.png through screen06.png, kaboom-2025.png, PokeANose_MediaImages.zip. Place the generated Instagram icon at frontend/public/assets/generated/instagram-icon-transparent.dim_64x64.png. Reference all assets using absolute public paths (e.g., /assets/gamelogo.png).

Fix the Internet Identity authentication flow on /admin so that after II login completes, the app no longer gets stuck in a 'verifying admin access' state. The root causes to address are: (1) disallowed origin errors from the II provider rejecting the app's origin — ensure the InternetIdentityProvider in frontend/src/main.tsx is configured with the correct `loginOptions` including the app's own origin in `derivationOrigin` or equivalent allowed-origins config; (2) the 'Detected both agent and agentOptions passed to createActor' warning in useActor.ts — refactor actor creation so that only `agent` OR `agentOptions` is passed to createActor, never both simultaneously; (3) after these fixes, confirm that the actor is created with the authenticated identity so `initializeAdmin()` receives the correct principal and the dashboard renders without hanging.

Update the Admin Dashboard (/admin) page so that after Internet Identity login succeeds, the full CMS editing dashboard is displayed immediately — without calling initializeAdmin() or any admin-verification step. Remove the 'verifying admin access' state, the access-denied branch, and any UI that references admin principal registration. The page flow must be: (1) not authenticated → show II login button; (2) authenticated → show CMS dashboard directly.

Update the /admin page frontend flow as follows: (1) Not authenticated → show Internet Identity login button (unchanged). (2) Authenticated → call getAdminStatus() to check registration state. (3) If adminClaimed is false, call claimAdmin() to register the current principal as the permanent admin, then show the full CMS dashboard. (4) If adminClaimed is true and callerIsAdmin is true, show the full CMS dashboard directly. (5) If adminClaimed is true and callerIsAdmin is false, show an 'Access Denied' message explaining that an admin is already registered, with no CMS controls visible. Show a loading/checking state while getAdminStatus() or claimAdmin() are in flight.

Remove any 'Poke A Nose' or game title text rendered as plain text in the site header (Header.tsx) on all pages. The header must contain only navigation links and the dark/light mode toggle — no game title text.

On the landing page (/), remove the newsletter/subscribe section (the email input, Subscribe button, and any surrounding 'Stay Tuned' or newsletter copy). Also remove the description paragraph 'A hand-drawn point-and-click adventure game full of absurd humor, quirky characters, and puzzles that will make you scratch your head — and maybe poke a nose or two.' The rest of the landing page (background, game logo, press kit button, footer) must remain unchanged.

On the landing page (/), make the game logo (gamelogo.png) display at its natural full size and be fully responsive: on desktop it should fill a large portion of the viewport width (minimum 400px, up to its natural maximum size), and on mobile/tablet it should scale down fluidly to fill the available width without cropping or overflow. Use max-width: 100% with an auto height so it never breaks the layout on any screen size.

On the press kit page (/press-kit), remove the hero section's plain text game title ('Poke A Nose' rendered as a heading) and the tagline text 'A hand-drawn point-and-click adventure' (or any variant). Replace them with the gamelogo.png image displayed prominently at the top of the press kit, using the same responsive sizing rules as the landing page logo (full natural width on desktop, scales down on mobile). Apply dark mode inversion (CSS filter invert) to this logo consistent with the rest of the site.

On the landing page (/), reduce the game logo (gamelogo.png) size so it appears noticeably smaller than its current size on desktop. It should still be responsive (scaling down on mobile/tablet with max-width: 100% and auto height), but the desktop maximum size must be reduced — use a max-width in the range of 480px–560px (down from the current full natural width) so it no longer dominates the viewport.

Add a YouTube Link field to the Admin Dashboard (/admin) CMS. It must be a text input pre-populated with the current youtubeLink value from getContent(), with a Save button that calls updateYoutubeLink() and then invalidates/refetches the content query. Add the corresponding useUpdateYoutubeLink mutation to frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts.

Update the VideoSection component on /press-kit to use the youtubeLink value from the global content query (returned by getContent()) instead of a hardcoded YouTube URL. When the user clicks the video thumbnail play button, the embedded YouTube iframe must use this admin-editable link.

Update the Admin Dashboard (/admin) frontend so that after Internet Identity login, the CMS dashboard is shown immediately to any authenticated user without any admin-claim or access-denied gating. Prominently display the authenticated caller's principal ID (as a text string) at the top of the CMS dashboard, labeled clearly as 'Your Principal ID', styled so it is easy to read and copy.

Update the /admin frontend (AdminDashboard.tsx) so that after Internet Identity login, the app checks whether the authenticated principal equals `r423e-lboyg-thldn-64zao-ew2h3-bh3v6-uouit-khwus-vkwn2-afrmu-rqe`. If it matches, display the full CMS dashboard. If it does not match, display an 'Access Denied' message with no CMS controls visible. Remove any claimAdmin() calls, getAdminStatus() calls, and the 'Your Principal ID' display block. The page flow must be: (1) not authenticated → show II login button; (2) authenticated and principal matches hardcoded admin → show full CMS dashboard; (3) authenticated but principal does not match → show Access Denied message.

Add an Iframe URL field to the Admin Dashboard (/admin) CMS panel. It must be a text input pre-populated with the current `iframeSrc` value from `getContent()`, with a Save button that calls `updateIframeSrc()` and then invalidates/refetches the content query. Add the corresponding `useUpdateIframeSrc` mutation to `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts`.

On the Press Kit page (/press-kit), render an `<iframe>` using the `iframeSrc` value from the global content query. The iframe must only be rendered when `iframeSrc` is a non-empty string. It must be displayed as a distinct section between the Video Section and the About the Game section. The iframe must be fully responsive (100% width, with a fixed aspect ratio such as 16:9), have no visible border, and respect the page's light/dark theme styling.

new_feature_requests.summary: Password protection system: admin can enable/disable, SHA-256 hashed password stored in stable storage, password gate modal on /press-kit
Dark/light mode toggle in header; dark mode inverts background image and logo/festival logo, forces white body text
Admin-selectable body text color (light mode only) saved to backend stable storage and applied globally
Public landing page (/) with large gamelogo.png, description text, newsletter placeholder, and link to /press-kit

Press kit page (/press-kit) with hero, video section (YouTube embed on thumbnail click), About, Features, Game Details, Media/Screenshots, Social Media, Developer, Press/Support, and footer festival logo
Screenshots gallery (screen01-screen06) with fullscreen viewer on click, 1px black border, and ZIP download button for PokeANose_MediaImages.zip
Admin dashboard (/admin) CMS: edit About, Features (max 4), Game Details, Instagram link, Developer link, Press email, body text color, and password protection settings

Backend stable storage and all required methods: initializeAdmin, verifyAdmin, enablePasswordProtection, disablePasswordProtection, verifyPassword, getContent, updateAbout, updateFeatures, updateGameDetails, updateInstagram, updateDeveloperWebsite, updatePressEmail, updateBodyTextColor
Instagram button shown only if valid link exists, left-aligned, icon-only (instagram-icon-transparent), theme-aware styling, opens in new tab

Implement a Motoko backend actor with all required stable variables: adminPrincipal (Option<Principal>), passwordEnabled (Bool), passwordHash (Text), bodyTextColorHex (Text), aboutText (Text), features ([Text] max 4), gameDetails (genre/platforms/releaseDate as Text), instagramLink (Text), developerWebsite (Text), pressEmail (Text). All variables must persist across canister upgrades using stable storage.

Implement enablePasswordProtection(password: Text) backend update method (admin only): hash the provided password using SHA-256, store the hash in stable passwordHash variable, set passwordEnabled to true. Reject with error if caller is not the admin.
Implement disablePasswordProtection() backend update method (admin only): set passwordEnabled to false and clear the stored passwordHash. Reject with error if caller is not the admin.

Implement verifyPassword(password: Text) backend query method: hash the provided password with SHA-256 and compare to the stored passwordHash. Return #ok(true) if match, #ok(false) if no match or protection is disabled.

Implement getContent() backend query method: return a record containing all content fields (aboutText, features, gameDetails, instagramLink, developerWebsite, pressEmail, bodyTextColorHex) plus the passwordEnabled flag. This method is publicly callable with no authentication required.

Implement all admin-only content update methods: updateAbout(text: Text), updateFeatures(features: [Text]) (reject if more than 4 items), updateGameDetails(genre: Text, platforms: Text, releaseDate: Text), updateInstagram(link: Text), updateDeveloperWebsite(link: Text), updatePressEmail(email: Text), updateBodyTextColor(colorHex: Text). Each method must verify the caller is the registered admin before persisting changes.

Implement claimAdmin() backend method: the very first authenticated II principal to call it becomes the permanent admin stored in stable storage. Subsequent calls are rejected. Only the claimed admin principal can call CMS update methods. The admin principal cannot be changed unless the canister is reset.

Update /admin frontend flow: after II login, call claimAdmin() if no admin is yet registered (first visitor). If claimAdmin succeeds or the current principal is already the admin, show the full CMS dashboard. If an admin is already registered and the current principal is NOT the admin, show an 'Access Denied' message with no CMS controls.

Implement a claimAdmin() backend method in main.mo: the very first authenticated Internet Identity principal to call it becomes the permanent admin, stored in a stable variable (e.g., adminPrincipal: ?Principal). Subsequent calls from any other principal must be rejected with an error (e.g., 'Admin already claimed'). If the caller is already the registered admin, the call should be idempotent (succeed without error). The adminPrincipal must persist across canister upgrades via stable storage and cannot be changed unless the canister is reset.

Enforce admin-only authorization on all CMS update methods in main.mo (updateAbout, updateFeatures, updateGameDetails, updateInstagram, updateDeveloperWebsite, updatePressEmail, updateBodyTextColor, enablePasswordProtection, disablePasswordProtection). Each method must verify that msg.caller equals the stored adminPrincipal before persisting changes; reject with an 'Access denied' error otherwise.

Add a getAdminStatus() backend query method that returns a record indicating whether an admin has been claimed and whether the caller is that admin. Example: { adminClaimed: Bool; callerIsAdmin: Bool }. This allows the frontend to determine the registration state without exposing the stored principal directly.

Remove 'Poke A Nose' text from the header on all pages. On the landing page (/): remove the newsletter/subscribe section and the description paragraph. Make the game logo (gamelogo.png) display at full size and be fully responsive on mobile and desktop. On /press-kit: remove the 'Poke A Nose' hero text and the 'A hand-drawn point-and-click adventure' tagline text, and show the gamelogo.png PNG instead.
Make the landing page game logo (gamelogo.png) slightly smaller — it is currently too large on desktop.

Add a YouTube video link field to the CMS: admin can edit the YouTube URL for the press kit video section via the admin dashboard. Store in backend stable storage and return via getContent(). The press kit video section must use this admin-editable link instead of a hardcoded URL.

Add a stable text variable `youtubeLink` to the backend actor in backend/main.mo, initialized to 'https://youtu.be/5in-hIASH08'. Include it in the record returned by getContent(). Add an admin-only update method updateYoutubeLink(link: Text) that verifies the caller is the registered admin before persisting the new value. Reject with 'Access denied' otherwise.

Hardcode the admin principal r423e-lboyg-thldn-64zao-ew2h3-bh3v6-uouit-khwus-vkwn2-afrmu-rqe in the backend. Restore admin-only authorization on all CMS update methods so only this principal can call them. Update the /admin frontend to show the CMS dashboard only to this principal and show 'Access Denied' to all others.

In backend/main.mo, hardcode the admin principal `r423e-lboyg-thldn-64zao-ew2h3-bh3v6-uouit-khwus-vkwn2-afrmu-rqe` as a compile-time constant (using `Principal.fromText("r423e-lboyg-thldn-64zao-ew2h3-bh3v6-uouit-khwus-vkwn2-afrmu-rqe")`). Remove any dynamic claimAdmin / getAdminStatus logic. The stable adminPrincipal variable should be initialized to `?Principal.fromText("r423e-lboyg-thldn-64zao-ew2h3-bh3v6-uouit-khwus-vkwn2-afrmu-rqe")` so it persists across upgrades. All CMS update methods (updateAbout, updateFeatures, updateGameDetails, updateInstagram, updateDeveloperWebsite, updatePressEmail, updateBodyTextColor, updateYoutubeLink, enablePasswordProtection, disablePasswordProtection) must enforce that `msg.caller == hardcodedAdmin` and reject with `#err("Access denied")` for any other caller.
Add an iframe to the site (location and content/URL not yet specified by user)

Add an iframe field to the backend CMS: add a stable text variable `iframeSrc` (initialized to an empty string `""`) in backend/main.mo. Include `iframeSrc` in the record returned by `getContent()`. Add an admin-only update method `updateIframeSrc(src: Text)` that verifies the caller equals the hardcoded admin principal before persisting the new value, and rejects with `#err("Access denied")` otherwise.
